name: Pedestrian agents (unruly crossers)
role: A second, non-vehicle agent class the cars must predict and avoid: stochastic walkers that jaywalk and cross roads unpredictably.
decision: BUILT (scripted v1) in smoothride/env/kinematic.py: M pedestrians random-walk with occasional darts and reflect off world bounds; cars observe the nearest pedestrian and a ped-hit applies a severe asymmetric penalty (w_ped) plus an APF proximity term (w_ped_prox). Next: give pedestrians their own policy for genuine unpredictability, and tie them to OSM crossings/foot network.
inputs: sidewalk / crossing geometry from OSM (foot network)
design_notes: Direct callback to the hotel-robot prior: predict where a moving person will be and avoid the space-time overlap.
Asymmetric penalty encodes that a pedestrian collision is categorically worse than a fender-bender.
Pedestrians render as a distinct color/marker so the artifact clearly shows cars yielding to people.
risks: too-unruly pedestrians make the task unlearnable -> tune unpredictability
extra agent class enlarges the neighbor computation (couples with spatial-hashing scale work)
